title: tissa diandra subsp. bracteata
intro: Tissa diandra subsp. bracteata, also known as the Himalayan Sweet Pepper, is a plant species found in the Himalayan regions, characterized by its showy yellow flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. Its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating digestive problems, making it a valuable herbal remedy in the region. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
